executiveSummary.text: FFAI remains exactly the kind of listed EV casualty the market stops giving second chances to. At $0.2115 with a market cap of roughly $45.6M and EPS of -$5.41, this is no longer a serious operating equity story. It is a distressed speculative stub. Bottom line: SELL - SPECULATIVE . There is no sensible long-only investment case here beyond punting on noise.

businessModel.text: Faraday Future was meant to be a premium EV company. In practice, the equity case is dominated by financing stress, execution failures and survival risk rather than vehicle economics or scaling credibility.

financialSnapshot.text: Live price: $0.2115 Market cap: $45.6M Day range: $0.2100 - $0.2427 P/E: N/A EPS: -$5.41 Beta: 5.98

recentCatalysts.text: No clean positive operating catalyst is visible in the live data. At this stage, capital markets and survival matter more than product narrative. Extreme volatility is the norm, not the exception.

thesisEvaluation.text: Bull (10%): a financing or corporate event triggers a temporary rebound. Base (40%): the equity continues to decay as a distressed micro-cap. Bear (50%): further dilution or breakdown destroys residual value. Weighted conviction: 18/100.

keyRisks.text: Survival risk: the central issue. Dilution risk: near-certain in distressed structures. Volatility risk: beta near 6 is a warning, not a feature.

risks.risk: Dilution risk: near-certain in distressed structures.
Volatility risk: beta near 6 is a warning, not a feature.

whoShouldOwn.text: Should own: almost nobody besides very short-term punters. Should avoid: everyone treating public markets like investing rather than gambling.

recommendation.text: SELL - SPECULATIVE at $0.2115. Distressed stub, not an investment.
